title: VoIP debug route exposes API keys to unauthenticated users
description: The /api/voip/debug endpoint is publicly accessible with NO authentication. It exposes the first 10 characters of TELNYX_API_KEY and the full TELNYX_CONNECTION_ID in the JSON response. It also makes live API calls to Telnyx using the production key. The comment says 'DELETE THIS after debugging is complete' but it was never removed. Any internet user can access this endpoint.
fix: Delete this file entirely. If a debug endpoint is needed, protect it behind withAdminGuard with requireMfa: true and only allow in development mode.

title: Super-admin impersonation relies on spoofable x-tenant-slug header
description: The impersonate endpoint checks `request.headers.get('x-tenant-slug') !== 'attitudes'` for super-admin authorization. This header is a client-supplied HTTP header that can be trivially spoofed by any authenticated user. Any user with a valid session can set `x-tenant-slug: attitudes` and impersonate any tenant owner. The route does not use withAdminGuard, has no CSRF protection, no rate limiting, and no Zod validation on the body.
fix: Replace header-based check with a proper super-admin verification: check user's actual tenantId from the session (trusted DB lookup), require OWNER role, use withAdminGuard with requireMfa: true, add Zod validation for the body, and add audit logging with alerting.

title: Assisted tenant setup relies on spoofable header and returns plaintext password
description: The assisted-setup endpoint (1) checks super-admin via the spoofable x-tenant-slug header (same as impersonate), (2) returns the temporary password in plaintext in the API response (line 248: tempPassword: tempPassword), (3) uses Math.random() for password generation which is not cryptographically secure, (4) has no Zod validation on the body, and (5) has no CSRF or rate limiting. This endpoint can create tenants, Stripe customers, and user accounts.
fix: 1. Replace header-based auth with proper withAdminGuard + super-admin DB check. 2. Use crypto.randomBytes() instead of Math.random() for temp passwords. 3. Do NOT return tempPassword in the response; instead send it only via email. 4. Add Zod schema for request body validation. 5. Add rate limiting (1 request/minute).

title: VoIP route returns SIP password in plaintext
description: The /api/voip/credentials endpoint returns sipPassword in plaintext in the JSON response (select: { sipPassword: true }). While the route does check for an authenticated session, SIP passwords should never be returned to the client. If this response is logged, cached, or intercepted, it exposes VoIP infrastructure credentials.
fix: Never return sipPassword to the client. If the client needs SIP credentials, use a short-lived token exchange pattern or return a JWT that the SIP gateway can validate. At minimum, exclude sipPassword from the select clause.

title: Admin tutorials route reads arbitrary files without auth or path traversal protection
description: The /api/admin/tutoriels/[...slug] endpoint has NO authentication (no withAdminGuard, no session check). While the regex /^[\w\-/]+$/ blocks '..' traversal (\w doesn't include dots), it still allows reading any .md file under docs/user-guide/ without authentication. Additionally, the directory structure and file existence is revealed to unauthenticated users via 404 vs success responses.
fix: Add withAdminGuard wrapper. Even though path traversal is blocked by the regex, this endpoint should not be publicly accessible as it reveals internal documentation structure.

title: Tenant management uses ad-hoc auth instead of withAdminGuard; POST has no Zod validation
description: The tenants route implements its own verifySuperAdmin() function instead of using withAdminGuard. This means it lacks: CSRF protection, rate limiting, body size validation, tenant context, and standardized error handling. The POST handler (line 87) accepts body fields without Zod validation and passes them directly to prisma.tenant.create, including potentially dangerous fields like modulesEnabled and featuresFlags.
fix: Refactor to use withAdminGuard with requiredPermission. Add Zod schema for POST body. The verifySuperAdmin() check (OWNER role) is also too broad - any OWNER can access, not just the platform super-admin.

title: Admin API guard trusts client-supplied x-tenant-slug header for tenant context
description: The withAdminGuard (line 144) reads x-tenant-slug from request headers and uses it to set tenant context. If the session doesn't have a tenantId, the header value is used to resolve the tenant. Since this header is client-supplied, any authenticated admin could potentially set it to access a different tenant's data. The x-tenant-super-admin header (line 145) is also client-supplied.
fix: Remove trust of x-tenant-slug and x-tenant-super-admin headers. Always resolve tenant context from the authenticated session's tenantId (which comes from the database). If cross-tenant access is needed for super-admins, validate that the user is actually a platform super-admin via a DB lookup, not a header.

title: Community debug route exposes Prisma model names and DB schema in development
description: The /api/community/debug endpoint reveals internal Prisma model names, database table structure (via pg_tables query), and NODE_ENV. While it blocks in production (returns 404), the production check relies on NODE_ENV which could be misconfigured. The endpoint exists at a predictable URL and reveals the 404 vs success distinction.
fix: Delete this file entirely. Debug information should be obtained through proper logging/monitoring, not exposed via API endpoints. If kept, add IP allowlist and authentication.

title: VoIP settings route merges full request body into stored settings
description: The PUT handler uses `{ ...current, ...body }` to merge the full request body into the current settings object, then stores it. This is a mass assignment vulnerability - an attacker could inject arbitrary fields into the settings object, potentially overwriting security-critical settings or injecting unexpected data.
fix: Add Zod validation schema that whitelists only the expected settings fields. Never spread the raw body into a stored object.

title: Promotions route spreads rawBody into Prisma create without full field whitelist
description: The POST handler uses `...rawBody` in the Prisma create call. While it goes through Zod validation, the Zod schema may use .passthrough() or accept extra fields that get passed directly to the database. The same pattern exists in PUT at /api/admin/promotions/[id]/route.ts line 129.
fix: Extract only the validated, whitelisted fields from the Zod output. Never spread raw or partially-validated body into Prisma operations.

title: 9 admin routes bypass withAdminGuard (missing CSRF, rate limiting, body size checks)
description: 9 routes under /api/admin/ do not use withAdminGuard, losing all its security layers: CSRF validation, rate limiting (Redis-backed), body size limits (1MB), role verification, tenant context, MFA enforcement, and standardized error handling. The routes are: (1) /admin/ads/cron/ - cron+session auth, (2) /admin/platform/assisted-setup/ - header-based auth, (3) /admin/platform/impersonate/ - header-based auth, (4) /admin/platform/tenants/ - custom verifySuperAdmin, (5) /admin/social-posts/cron/ - cron+session auth, (6) /admin/tutoriels/[...slug]/ - NO auth at all, (7) /admin/tutoriels/magazine/[...slug]/ - session only, (8) /admin/voip/cdr/ingest/ - webhook secret auth, (9) /admin/voip/surveys/submit/ - webhook secret auth. Routes 8-9 are webhooks and appropriately use shared-secret auth.
fix: Routes 1-5 should use withAdminGuard with appropriate options. Route 6 needs auth added. Route 7 already has auth() but should use withAdminGuard for consistency. Routes 8-9 are webhooks and are acceptable as-is.

title: 50 admin routes use hard delete (prisma.delete) instead of soft delete
description: 50 DELETE operations in admin routes permanently destroy records using prisma.*.delete(). This violates data retention requirements, makes audit trails incomplete, and prevents data recovery. Affected models include: CrmLead, CrmDeal, CrmCampaign, CrmQuote, CrmQuota, CrmPlaybook, CrmDealTeam, CrmDealProduct, Prospect, ProspectList, Article, EmailTemplate, EmailAccount, EmailCampaign, EmailAutomationFlow, Supplier, PurchaseOrder, Webinar, LoyaltyTierConfig, ShippingZone, Media, FAQ, Page, Currency, PermissionGroup, AdminNavPage, AdminNavSection, OptionTypeOption. Sample files: src/app/api/admin/crm/deals/[id]/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/crm/leads/[id]/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/suppliers/[id]/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/articles/[id]/route.ts.
fix: Replace prisma.*.delete() with soft delete: update the record to set deletedAt: new Date() and isActive: false. Add deletedAt field to models that lack it. Update all list queries to filter WHERE deletedAt IS NULL.

title: VoIP SMS webhook has no authentication or signature verification
description: The /api/voip/webhooks/sms route has no session check, no auth guard, no webhook secret verification, and no HMAC signature validation. It appears to accept arbitrary SMS webhook payloads without verifying they come from Telnyx. Compare with /api/webhooks/sms-inbound which properly verifies HMAC signatures.
fix: Add Telnyx webhook signature verification using HMAC-SHA256 with the TELNYX_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Reject requests with invalid or missing signatures.

title: Product viewed tracking has no auth - potential for data injection
description: The /api/products/viewed endpoint is publicly accessible without authentication. If it accepts POST requests to record product views, an attacker could inject arbitrary view data to manipulate analytics, recommendation algorithms, and 'trending' products.
fix: Add rate limiting per IP. If the endpoint accepts writes, require at least a valid session token or anonymous session ID with rate limiting.

title: 753 routes (82%) lack CSRF protection
description: Only 166 out of 919 routes implement CSRF token validation. While routes using withAdminGuard get CSRF protection automatically for mutation methods, many non-admin routes that accept POST/PUT/PATCH requests do not validate CSRF tokens. This includes auth routes (login, register, signup), account routes, cart routes, community routes, and public-facing routes. Note: withAdminGuard handles CSRF for its 615 wrapped routes, so the effective unprotected count for admin mutation routes is low, but non-admin mutation routes remain exposed.
fix: For non-admin mutation routes, implement CSRF validation using the existing validateCsrf() middleware from @/lib/csrf-middleware. At minimum, protect: auth routes (login, register, signup, forgot-password, reset-password), account mutation routes, cart/checkout routes, and community post routes.

title: 736 routes (80%) lack explicit rate limiting
description: Only 183 out of 919 routes implement rate limiting. While withAdminGuard provides rate limiting for 615 admin routes (Redis-backed with in-memory fallback), many public-facing and account routes lack rate limiting. Auth routes (login, register, forgot-password, signup, reset-password) DO have rate limiting, which is good. But account endpoints, cart endpoints, product endpoints, community endpoints, and public API endpoints do not. Sample unprotected high-value routes: /api/account/password, /api/account/profile, /api/cart/*, /api/checkout/*, /api/community/posts/*.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware to all public-facing mutation endpoints. Priority: checkout routes, account mutation routes, contact/demo-request forms, public chatbot routes. Use the existing rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter.

title: 395 routes (43%) lack Zod input validation
description: 395 out of 919 routes do not use Zod schemas for input validation. 85 of these are mutation routes (POST/PUT/PATCH) that accept user input without schema validation. Breakdown by area: admin (199 routes), accounting (54), cron (29), account (16), v1 API (8), products (7), platform (7), voip (6), auth (6). Many GET-only routes legitimately don't need Zod (they only read query params), but all mutation routes should validate their bodies.
fix: Add Zod schemas to all 85 mutation routes that lack them. Priority: platform routes (tenant creation, domain setup, billing), accounting mutation routes (journal entries, invoices), auth routes (login, oauth), and any route that writes to the database.

title: 77 routes lack try/catch error handling
description: 77 out of 919 routes lack try/catch blocks. Routes wrapped in withAdminGuard get error handling from the guard itself, but routes without guards that also lack try/catch will crash with unhandled exceptions, potentially exposing stack traces in development or returning generic 500 errors. Sample routes without error handling: src/app/api/v1/customers/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/customers/[id]/notes/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/products/[id]/sales/route.ts, src/app/api/admin/crm/qa-forms/route.ts.
fix: Add try/catch blocks to all 77 routes. For routes using withAdminGuard, the guard's outer try/catch provides coverage, so focus on routes that don't use any guard wrapper.

title: Public chat route accessible without auth, rate limiting, or CSRF
description: The /api/public/chat and /api/public/chatbot endpoints are completely unprotected - no authentication, no rate limiting, no CSRF. These endpoints likely accept user input (chat messages) and could be abused for spam, denial of service, or injection attacks.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP), input length validation, and basic abuse detection. Consider adding a simple CAPTCHA or proof-of-work challenge for anonymous users.

title: Demo request form has no auth, rate limiting, or CSRF protection
description: The /api/demo-request endpoint is a public form submission endpoint with no protections. It can be abused for: spam (submitting thousands of fake demo requests), email bombing (if it sends confirmation emails), and resource exhaustion.
fix: Add rate limiting (5 requests/minute per IP), Zod validation for input, and consider adding a honeypot field or reCAPTCHA integration.

title: Click tracking endpoint has no auth or rate limiting
description: The /api/tracking/click and /api/tracking/email endpoints are publicly accessible without any protections. An attacker could: inflate click analytics, forge email open tracking, manipulate campaign metrics, or cause excessive database writes.
fix: Add rate limiting per IP. Validate that the tracking token/ID is a valid format. Consider using signed URLs with HMAC to prevent fabricated tracking events.

title: Mailing list confirmation has no rate limiting
description: The /api/mailing-list/confirm endpoint is publicly accessible without rate limiting. An attacker could brute-force confirmation tokens or cause excessive database lookups.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure confirmation tokens are sufficiently random (at least 32 bytes) and have an expiry.

title: Consent token endpoint has no rate limiting or token format validation
description: The /api/consent/[token] endpoint processes consent tokens without rate limiting. Token-based endpoints are vulnerable to brute-force enumeration if tokens are short or predictable.
fix: Add rate limiting. Validate token format before database lookup. Ensure tokens are cryptographically random and at least 32 bytes.

title: Estimates token endpoint accessible without auth or rate limiting
description: The /api/estimates/[token] endpoint allows access to estimates/quotes via a token URL. Without rate limiting, an attacker could enumerate tokens to access business estimates containing pricing and client information.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure tokens are cryptographically secure (UUID v4 minimum). Add token expiry if not already present.

title: 251 POST routes return 200 instead of 201 for resource creation
description: Out of 466 POST handlers, only 215 return status 201 for resource creation. The remaining 251 return 200, which violates HTTP semantics. While not a security vulnerability per se, incorrect status codes can mask issues in monitoring, cause confusion in API consumers, and make audit logging less precise.
fix: Review all POST handlers that create resources and change their success status to 201. POST handlers that perform actions (not creation) can keep 200.

title: 33 routes have zero security controls (no auth, CSRF, rate limit, or secret)
description: 33 routes have absolutely no security mechanism. Most are legitimately public (product listings, search, FAQ, health check), but several should have protections: /api/public/chat, /api/public/chatbot, /api/demo-request, /api/tracking/click, /api/tracking/email, /api/mailing-list/confirm, /api/consent/[token], /api/estimates/[token], /api/data-deletion, /api/admin/tutoriels/[...slug], /api/voip/debug, /api/community/debug. Full list: bundles/*, client-references, contact/platforms, currencies, faq, guides, hero-slides/active, payment-methods, products/related, products/by-slug, products/compare, products/recommendations, search/popular, search/suggest, testimonials, videos/*, webinars.
fix: Add at minimum rate limiting to all 33 routes. Add authentication to debug routes and admin routes. Add CSRF to any that accept POST.

title: withAdminGuard is not used by 304 routes (33% of total) including all non-admin routes
description: The withAdminGuard provides comprehensive security (auth, role check, CSRF, rate limiting, body size, error handling, tenant context). 615 routes use it (all in admin/), but 304 routes outside admin/ have no equivalent guard. Non-admin mutation routes (account updates, cart operations, checkout, community posts, etc.) rely on ad-hoc session checks without CSRF, rate limiting, or standardized error handling. There is no equivalent 'withUserGuard' or 'withPublicGuard' wrapper.
fix: Create a withUserGuard middleware for authenticated non-admin routes (provides: session check, CSRF for mutations, rate limiting, error handling). Create a withPublicGuard for anonymous routes (provides: rate limiting, error handling, body size limits). Migrate all routes to use the appropriate guard.

title: Accounting routes (134) mix withAdminGuard and custom auth patterns
description: The 134 accounting routes use a mix of: withAdminGuard (majority), custom requireAdmin/requireAccountant from @/lib/accounting/auth-middleware (2 routes), validatePortalAccess for client portal (4 routes), and cron secret auth (1 route). This inconsistency means some accounting routes get full security (CSRF, rate limit, body size) while others don't. The auth-middleware.ts has its own role hierarchy (ADMIN/ACCOUNTANT/VIEWER) that's separate from withAdminGuard's permission system.
fix: Standardize all accounting routes to use withAdminGuard with requiredPermission options from the permissions system. Migrate the custom accounting role checks to withAdminGuard's permission system.

title: V1 public API routes (10) lack comprehensive security despite using withApiAuth
description: The 10 v1/ API routes use withApiAuth for API key validation, which is good. However, they lack: Zod input validation (8 of 10 have no Zod), CSRF protection, rate limiting (API keys should still have per-key rate limits), and try/catch error handling in some routes. These are public-facing API routes that third parties use, making them high-value targets.
fix: Add Zod validation to all v1 POST routes. Add per-API-key rate limiting. Ensure all routes have proper error handling.

title: Account routes (30) use ad-hoc session checks without standardized security
description: The 30 routes under /api/account/ handle sensitive user operations (password change, profile update, data export, delete request, MFA setup, order management, wishlists). They use individual session checks rather than a standardized guard, meaning they lack: consistent CSRF validation, rate limiting, body size limits, and standardized error responses. These are high-value targets for CSRF attacks (e.g., changing someone's password by tricking them into visiting a malicious page).
fix: Create withUserGuard that wraps session verification + CSRF + rate limiting. Apply it to all 30 account routes. Priority: password, profile, MFA, delete-request, data-export routes.

title: 85 mutation routes (POST/PUT/PATCH) accept user input without Zod validation
description: 85 routes that accept POST/PUT/PATCH requests do not use Zod schemas to validate the request body. This means arbitrary JSON can be passed to these handlers. Combined with spread operators in some handlers, this creates mass assignment vulnerabilities. Affected areas: platform routes (7 routes handling tenant/subscription/billing), accounting (14 mutation routes), auth (6 routes including login and oauth), admin misc (30+ routes), voip (6 routes). Sample critical unvalidated mutations: /api/platform/provision, /api/platform/checkout, /api/platform/licenses, /api/platform/domain, /api/auth/oauth, /api/auth/login.
fix: Add Zod schemas to all 85 mutation routes. Start with the most critical: platform management, auth, and any route that creates/modifies user records or financial data.

title: 55 hard-delete operations in accounting routes risk data loss and audit trail gaps
description: 5 accounting routes use prisma.*.delete() for permanent data removal: accountingPeriod.delete, bankRule.delete, workflow.delete, documentAttachment.delete, projectMilestone.delete. For financial/accounting data, hard deletes can violate regulatory requirements (SOX, CPA retention rules, Loi 25), break audit trails, and prevent data recovery. Combined with the 50 hard deletes in admin routes, this totals 55 permanent delete operations across the codebase.
fix: Replace all accounting hard deletes with soft deletes (set deletedAt, keep record). Financial records should NEVER be permanently deleted. For admin routes, implement soft delete for business-critical entities (CRM leads, deals, campaigns, suppliers, articles) and allow hard delete only for ephemeral entities (nav config, option types).

title: Webhook routes (13) have inconsistent signature verification patterns
description: The 13 webhook routes use varied authentication patterns: HMAC-SHA256 with timing-safe comparison (stripe/paypal/meta/teams/zapier/shipping/email-bounce/email-inbound/sms-inbound - good), Webex signature verification via library (webex - good), Twilio signature when header present (whatsapp - conditional), no verification (voip/webhooks/sms - bad), and gone/disabled (stripe duplicate - fine). The inconsistency means some webhooks could accept forged requests.
fix: Ensure ALL webhook routes verify the sender's signature. Specifically fix /api/voip/webhooks/sms to verify Telnyx webhook signatures. Make the WhatsApp webhook's Twilio signature check mandatory (not conditional on header presence).

title: Cron routes use inconsistent auth patterns (some via CRON_SECRET, some via withAdminGuard)
description: Cron routes under /api/cron/ and /api/admin/*/cron/ use different auth strategies. Some accept CRON_SECRET Bearer token OR admin session (good dual auth), while others only check session. If CRON_SECRET is not set, some cron routes fall back to requiring admin session only, which means automated cron execution fails silently. 29 cron routes need standardized auth.
fix: Create a withCronGuard middleware that standardizes: CRON_SECRET validation (timing-safe), fallback to admin session, proper logging, and rate limiting. Apply to all 29 cron routes.

title: Search and product endpoints lack rate limiting (DoS risk)
description: The /api/search/suggest, /api/search/popular, /api/products/compare, /api/products/recommendations, and /api/products/[id]/related routes are public, have no authentication, and no rate limiting. These endpoints likely execute database queries that could be expensive. An attacker could flood these endpoints to cause database overload or increase cloud costs.
fix: Add rate limiting to all search and product listing endpoints. Suggested limits: 30 requests/minute per IP for search, 60 requests/minute per IP for product reads. Use the existing rateLimitMiddleware.

title: Chat conversation update uses spread operator on validated data (potential mass assignment)
description: The DELETE handler at line 263 uses prisma.conversation.delete (hard delete). The PUT handler at line 208 uses `const updateData: Record<string, unknown> = { ...data }` which spreads potentially untrusted data into the update operation.
fix: Replace hard delete with soft delete. In the PUT handler, explicitly whitelist the fields that can be updated rather than spreading the data object.

title: Client portal token routes (4) lack rate limiting for token enumeration protection
description: The 4 accounting client-portal routes (/api/accounting/client-portal/[token]/invoices, /payments, /estimates, /statement) use token-based access (validatePortalAccess). While tokens provide auth, there is no rate limiting to prevent brute-force token enumeration. If tokens are short or sequential, attackers could access other clients' financial data.
fix: Add rate limiting (10 requests/minute per IP). Ensure portal tokens are UUIDv4 or longer (128+ bits of entropy). Add token expiry and revocation. Log failed token lookups for monitoring.

title: Auth routes lack CSRF protection on mutation endpoints
description: Auth routes (/api/auth/login, /api/auth/register, /api/auth/signup, /api/auth/forgot-password, /api/auth/reset-password, /api/auth/accept-invite) accept POST requests without CSRF token validation. While these routes do have rate limiting (which mitigates brute force), the lack of CSRF means an attacker could forge requests on behalf of a user who visits a malicious page (login CSRF, forced registration, unwanted password reset emails).
fix: Add CSRF validation to all auth mutation endpoints using validateCsrf() from @/lib/csrf-middleware. Login CSRF is particularly dangerous as it can force a victim to log into the attacker's account.

title: CRM quotas route uses spread operator on data without full validation
description: Line 25 shows `...data` being spread into a Prisma operation. If the data object contains fields not in the Zod schema (or if .passthrough() is used), extra fields could be injected into the database.
fix: Destructure only expected fields from validated data. Never spread validated data directly if the Zod schema might allow extra fields.

title: VoIP CDR ingest allows dev-mode bypass of webhook authentication
description: The /api/admin/voip/cdr/ingest route (line 40-46) returns true when VOIP_CDR_WEBHOOK_SECRET is not set AND NODE_ENV is not 'production'. This means in staging/preview environments, any unauthenticated request can inject CDR data. The survey submit route has the same pattern.
fix: Remove the dev-mode bypass. Require webhook secret in all environments. Use a .env file with a dev secret for local development.

title: JSON.parse without try/catch in 10 accounting routes risks unhandled exceptions
description: 10 instances of JSON.parse() in accounting routes are not wrapped in their own try/catch blocks. While the outer route handler may have a try/catch, malformed JSON stored in database fields (notes, resultData, errorLog, inputData, config) will cause the JSON.parse to throw, potentially interrupting the response with an unhelpful error. Files: src/app/api/accounting/tax-reports/route.ts (lines 312, 330), src/app/api/accounting/batch/[id]/route.ts (lines 34, 40, 46), src/app/api/accounting/reports/custom/route.ts, src/app/api/accounting/reports/custom/[id]/route.ts, src/app/api/accounting/reports/custom/[id]/run/route.ts.
fix: Wrap each JSON.parse in a try/catch with a sensible fallback (empty object or null). Create a safeParse helper function.

title: Product import route spreads data into Prisma create
description: The product import handler at line 355 uses `...data` in Prisma create. Import endpoints are particularly risky because they process bulk data from external sources (CSV/Excel files) which could contain unexpected fields.
fix: Explicitly map only the expected fields from the parsed import data. Never spread imported data directly into database operations.

title: withAdminGuard has no automated test suite despite protecting 615 routes
description: The admin-api-guard.ts is the single most security-critical file in the codebase, protecting 615 routes. It implements auth, role checks, CSRF, rate limiting, tenant context, MFA enforcement, body size limits, and error handling. There should be comprehensive unit tests and integration tests for: (1) unauthenticated access returns 401, (2) non-admin roles return 403, (3) CSRF validation blocks invalid tokens on mutations, (4) rate limiting returns 429, (5) body size > 1MB returns 413, (6) MFA enforcement for OWNER roles, (7) permission checks, (8) tenant context resolution, (9) error handling doesn't leak details.
fix: Create src/__tests__/lib/admin-api-guard.test.ts with test cases for all 9 security layers. Mock auth(), validateCsrf(), checkRateLimit(), and prisma. Test both success and failure paths.

title: No integration tests for CSRF validation across route types
description: CSRF protection is implemented but there are no tests verifying that: (1) mutation requests without CSRF tokens are rejected, (2) GET requests bypass CSRF (correctly), (3) the CSRF token is properly validated against the session. Without tests, CSRF protection could silently break during refactoring.
fix: Create integration tests that make POST/PUT/DELETE requests to sample routes both with and without CSRF tokens, verifying 403 responses for missing/invalid tokens.

title: No tests verifying auth guards are applied to all admin routes
description: There are 9 admin routes that don't use withAdminGuard. There should be an automated test (or CI check) that scans all route files under /api/admin/ and verifies they either use withAdminGuard or are explicitly exempted (webhooks, cron with CRON_SECRET). This prevents future routes from accidentally being deployed without auth.
fix: Create a CI script or test that: finds all route.ts files under src/app/api/admin/, checks each one imports and uses withAdminGuard, and fails if any route is unprotected without being in an explicit allowlist. Pattern: grep -rL 'withAdminGuard' src/app/api/admin/ --include='route.ts'

title: No tests for rate limiting effectiveness across deployment restarts
description: The admin-api-guard uses Redis-backed rate limiting with in-memory fallback. There are no tests verifying: (1) Redis rate limiting works correctly across multiple requests, (2) the fallback to in-memory works when Redis is down, (3) rate limit headers are correctly set in responses, (4) the 429 response is returned after the limit is exceeded. Given that rate limiting had failures in the past (FAILLE-009), this needs automated verification.
fix: Create tests that: mock Redis client for both available and unavailable scenarios, send multiple requests to verify rate limit enforcement, verify Retry-After and X-RateLimit-* headers, and test the memory fallback path.

title: No tests for webhook signature verification across all webhook routes
description: 13 webhook routes implement various signature verification methods (HMAC-SHA256, Svix signatures, Twilio signatures, Webex/Zoom platform-specific). There are no tests verifying that: (1) valid signatures are accepted, (2) invalid signatures are rejected, (3) missing signatures are rejected, (4) timing-safe comparison is actually used. A single bug in signature verification could allow forged webhooks.
fix: Create webhook-auth.test.ts that tests each webhook route's signature verification with valid, invalid, and missing signatures. Verify timing-safe comparison is used (not simple === equality).

title: No tests for Zod schema completeness on critical mutation routes
description: While many routes have Zod schemas, there are no tests verifying that: (1) schemas reject unexpected fields (no .passthrough()), (2) schemas enforce proper types and lengths, (3) schemas handle edge cases (empty strings, null, undefined, very long inputs). Critical schemas to test: createCustomerSchema, updateCustomerSchema, mergeSchema, tenant creation, order creation, invoice creation.
fix: Create Zod schema tests for each critical model. Test with: valid input, missing required fields, extra fields (should be stripped), boundary values (max length strings, negative numbers), and injection attempts (SQL in strings, HTML in text fields).

title: No regression tests for previously fixed security vulnerabilities (FAILLE-*)
description: The codebase references multiple fixed security vulnerabilities: FAILLE-002 (permission bypass), FAILLE-009 (rate limiting), FAILLE-022 (error message exposure), FAILLE-055 (IP spoofing), FAILLE-066 (body size), FAILLE-078 (rate limit bucket). There are no automated tests ensuring these fixes remain in place during future refactoring.
fix: Create security-regression.test.ts with test cases for each FAILLE-* fix: (1) FAILLE-002: non-permitted user gets 403, (2) FAILLE-009: rate limiter uses Redis, (3) FAILLE-022: 500 errors don't expose messages, (4) FAILLE-055: IP extraction uses rightmost XFF, (5) FAILLE-066: >1MB body returns 413, (6) FAILLE-078: dynamic path segments are normalized.

title: No tests verifying DELETE routes properly check resource ownership
description: 130 DELETE handlers exist across the codebase. While most are behind withAdminGuard, there are no tests verifying that: (1) users can only delete their own resources, (2) cross-tenant deletion is blocked, (3) cascading deletes don't affect other tenants' data. In a multi-tenant system, this is critical.
fix: Create ownership-check tests for DELETE endpoints, especially: account routes (can user A delete user B's wishlist?), and admin routes in multi-tenant context (can tenant A's admin delete tenant B's data?).

title: No security scanning in CI/CD for new routes missing guards
description: The codebase has 919 routes and growing. There is no automated check in the CI/CD pipeline that verifies new routes meet minimum security requirements (auth guard, input validation, error handling). This means developers can accidentally push unprotected routes to production.
fix: Add a CI step that runs a script checking: (1) all admin routes use withAdminGuard, (2) all mutation routes have Zod validation, (3) all routes have try/catch or a guard wrapper, (4) no debug routes exist outside development. The script should fail the build if violations are found.

title: No API response schema validation tests
description: While input validation (Zod) is applied to 524 routes, there are no tests verifying that API responses match expected schemas. This means: (1) sensitive fields could accidentally be included in responses (password, tokens), (2) response format changes could break API consumers, (3) v1 API responses have no contract tests.
fix: Create response schema tests for critical API endpoints, especially v1/ public API routes and any route that returns user data. Verify no sensitive fields (password, mfaSecret, apiKeys) leak in responses.

title: Temporary password uses Math.random() which is not cryptographically secure
description: Line 100: `const tempPassword = \`Koraline-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(2, 10)}\``. Math.random() is not a CSPRNG (Cryptographically Secure Pseudo-Random Number Generator). The generated passwords are predictable if the internal state of the PRNG is known or can be sampled. This is a tenant owner's initial password.
fix: Use crypto.randomBytes(16).toString('hex') or crypto.randomUUID() for generating temporary passwords. Example: `const tempPassword = \`Koraline-${crypto.randomBytes(8).toString('hex')}\``;

title: Data deletion endpoint has no authentication
description: The /api/data-deletion route handles GDPR/Loi 25 data deletion requests with no authentication whatsoever. An attacker could submit fake deletion requests, potentially triggering the deletion of user data. This endpoint is legally required (Meta data deletion callback) but should still validate the request source.
fix: For Meta data deletion callbacks, verify the signed_request parameter using the APP_SECRET. For direct user requests, require authentication. Add rate limiting.

title: VoIP routes (36) include 2 unprotected endpoints beyond webhooks
description: Out of 36 VoIP routes, 2 have no auth: /api/voip/debug (API key exposure, discussed in C1-SEC-S-051) and /api/voip/webhooks/sms (no signature verification, discussed in C1-SEC-S-063). The remaining 34 routes properly use auth(). However, the /api/voip/credentials route returns sipPassword in plaintext (discussed in C1-SEC-S-054).
fix: Delete /api/voip/debug, add signature verification to /api/voip/webhooks/sms, and stop returning sipPassword from /api/voip/credentials.

title: Platform routes (11) handle critical operations with inconsistent security
description: The 11 platform routes handle tenant management, billing, licensing, domain setup, and health checks. They use a mix of: withAdminGuard (some), custom auth() + header checks (impersonate, assisted-setup, tenants), and no auth (health). The impersonate and assisted-setup routes are the most dangerous and have the weakest auth (header-based). The provision, checkout, and billing-portal routes handle Stripe operations and lack Zod validation.
fix: Migrate all platform management routes to use withAdminGuard with requiredPermission: 'platform.manage'. Add Zod validation to provision, checkout, and billing-portal routes. Fix the header-based auth in impersonate and assisted-setup.

title: 59 routes use Prisma $queryRaw but all use parameterized queries (no SQL injection found)
description: 59 routes use $queryRaw or $executeRaw for raw SQL queries. No instances of $queryRawUnsafe or $executeRawUnsafe were found. All raw queries appear to use Prisma.sql template literals which automatically parameterize values, preventing SQL injection. This is a positive finding.
fix: No fix needed. Continue using Prisma.sql template literals. Add a linting rule to block $queryRawUnsafe and $executeRawUnsafe imports.

title: Auth routes (18) properly implement rate limiting (positive finding)
description: All 5 critical auth routes (login, register, signup, forgot-password, reset-password) implement rate limiting. The login route also includes brute-force protection. This is a positive finding that shows good security practices for the most attacked endpoints.
fix: No fix needed. Consider adding CSRF protection as noted in C1-COMP-A-062.

title: Magazine tutorial route has weak path sanitization
description: Line 21 uses `slugPath.replace(/\.\./g, '').replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_\-\/\.]/g, '')` which removes '..' after the fact but allows dots in the final path. The sanitization is applied after joining the slug array, and the replacement of '..' could be bypassed with encodings like '..%2F' in some configurations. While the route does have auth (session check), the sanitization approach is fragile.
fix: Use path.resolve() and verify the resolved path starts with the expected base directory. Better: use a whitelist approach - map slugs to known file paths rather than constructing paths from user input.

title: withAdminGuard completeness review: well-implemented but has header trust issue
description: The admin-api-guard.ts is well-architected with 7 security layers: (1) Authentication via auth(), (2) Body size limits (1MB), (3) Role check (EMPLOYEE/OWNER), (4) Permission check (optional), (5) MFA enforcement (optional), (6) CSRF validation for mutations, (7) Rate limiting (Redis with memory fallback). Issues found: (a) trusts x-tenant-slug and x-tenant-super-admin headers for tenant context (C1-SEC-S-057), (b) ADMIN_ROLES allows EMPLOYEE which may be too broad for some operations, (c) IP extraction regex is generous (/^[\d.:a-fA-F]{3,45}$/).
fix: Fix the header trust issue (C1-SEC-S-057). Consider narrowing ADMIN_ROLES for sensitive operations via requiredPermission. Tighten the IP regex to match only valid IPv4/IPv6 formats.

title: No load testing for rate-limited endpoints to verify limits work under concurrency
description: Rate limiting is implemented on 183 routes but there are no load tests verifying that limits hold under concurrent requests. Redis-backed rate limiting could have race conditions under high concurrency that allow burst traffic past the limits.
fix: Create a load test script that sends concurrent requests to rate-limited endpoints and verifies the correct number are allowed through. Use artillery or k6 for this purpose.

title: Change password route selects password hash from database (acceptable pattern)
description: Line 49 selects `{ password: true }` from the user record to verify the current password via bcrypt.compare(). This is the correct pattern - the password hash is needed for verification and is never returned to the client. This is a positive finding confirming no data leak.
fix: No fix needed. The password hash is correctly used for server-side verification only.

title: Hard delete: CRM deal permanently deleted
description: Route uses prisma.crmDeal.delete() for permanent deletion. Deals contain revenue pipeline data, activity history, and product associations. Hard delete destroys forecasting accuracy and audit trails.
fix: Replace prisma.crmDeal.delete() with soft delete: prisma.crmDeal.update({ where: { id }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } }). Add deletedAt field to CrmDeal model if not present.

title: Hard delete: CRM lead permanently deleted
description: Route uses prisma.crmLead.delete() for permanent deletion. Leads represent potential customers. Hard delete prevents lead source attribution analysis and re-engagement campaigns.
fix: Replace prisma.crmLead.delete() with soft delete: prisma.crmLead.update({ where: { id }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } }). Add deletedAt field to CrmLead model if not present.

title: Hard delete: CRM contact permanently deleted via crmLead.delete
description: Route uses prisma.crmLead.delete() for permanent deletion. Deletes the lead record for a contact. This is destructive and may orphan related activities, notes, and communications.
fix: Replace prisma.crmLead.delete() with soft delete: prisma.crmLead.update({ where: { id }, data: { deletedAt: new Date() } }). Add deletedAt field to CrmLead model if not present.

title: Hard delete of financial record: AccountingPeriod
description: Route permanently deletes AccountingPeriod records. Accounting periods are regulatory artifacts. Deleting them breaks fiscal year audit trails and compliance reporting.
fix: Replace with soft delete. Financial records should NEVER be permanently deleted per CPA/SOX retention requirements.

title: Hard delete of financial record: BankRule
description: Route permanently deletes BankRule records. Bank reconciliation rules contain matching logic that may be needed for audit verification.
fix: Replace with soft delete. Financial records should NEVER be permanently deleted per CPA/SOX retention requirements.

title: Hard delete of financial record: PurchaseOrder

fix: Replace with soft delete. Financial records should NEVER be permanently deleted per CPA/SOX retention requirements.

title: Hard delete of financial record: Supplier
description: Route permanently deletes Supplier records. Supplier records are linked to purchase orders, invoices, and payments. Hard delete may orphan financial records.
fix: Replace with soft delete. Financial records should NEVER be permanently deleted per CPA/SOX retention requirements.

title: No Zod validation: Tenant provisioning accepts unvalidated body
description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Creates tenants and Stripe subscriptions from raw request body without schema validation. Could inject unexpected tenant configuration.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

description: POST handler lacks Zod schema validation. Handles Stripe checkout session creation without validating the plan, modules, or pricing parameters.
fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

fix: Add a Zod schema that validates all expected fields with proper types, lengths, and formats. Use .safeParse() and return 400 on validation failure.

title: No try/catch: V1 public API customers endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Public API routes must have robust error handling to prevent leaking internal errors to external consumers. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No try/catch: V1 public API products endpoint
description: Route handler lacks try/catch error handling. Unhandled Prisma errors could expose database schema details to API consumers. Unhandled exceptions return Next.js default 500 error which may expose internal details.
fix: Wrap the handler logic in try/catch. In catch, log the error with logger.error() and return a generic error response: NextResponse.json({ error: 'Internal server error' }, { status: 500 }).

title: No rate limiting: Contact platforms listing
description: Route lacks rate limiting. Reveals configured communication platforms without rate limiting.
fix: Add rateLimitMiddleware from @/lib/rate-limiter. Suggested limit depends on endpoint type: sensitive operations (5-10/min), read operations (30-60/min).

description: Route lacks rate limiting. Cart deletion endpoint could be used for DoS against user carts.

title: No E2E tests for public-facing API endpoints
description: Public endpoints (products, search, cart, reviews) have no end-to-end tests verifying they work correctly under various conditions. These are the most user-facing routes and failures directly impact revenue.
fix: Create E2E test suite covering: product listing, search, cart add/remove, checkout flow, review submission, and order tracking.

title: No tests for multi-tenant data isolation in API routes
description: In a multi-tenant system with 919 routes, there are no tests verifying that tenant A's API requests cannot access tenant B's data. The withAdminGuard sets tenant context, but there are no automated tests proving this isolation works correctly across all routes.
fix: Create multi-tenant isolation tests: create two tenants with test data, authenticate as each, and verify cross-tenant data is inaccessible via API calls.

title: No tests for API response time/performance budgets
description: No performance tests exist for API routes. Database-heavy routes (search, accounting reports, CRM analytics) could degrade under load. Without performance baselines, regressions go undetected.
fix: Add performance budget tests for critical routes: product search < 200ms, admin dashboard < 500ms, accounting reports < 2s. Alert on regressions.

title: SIP extension hard delete exposes VoIP infrastructure to misconfiguration
description: Deleting SIP extensions via prisma.sipExtension.delete() immediately invalidates VoIP routing. If an extension is deleted while calls are active, those calls will fail. No grace period or pre-flight check for active calls.
fix: Replace with soft delete. Before deletion, check for active calls on the extension. Implement a grace period before hard cleanup.

title: Phone number hard delete could orphan Telnyx resource
description: Deleting phone number record via prisma.phoneNumber.delete() removes the DB record but may not release the number at Telnyx, creating a billing orphan. Conversely, if the Telnyx API call fails after DB delete, the state is inconsistent.
fix: Implement two-phase delete: first release at Telnyx, then soft-delete the DB record. Use a transaction to ensure consistency.

title: Payment method config hard delete
description: PaymentMethodConfig records are permanently deleted. If a payment method is disabled by mistake, there is no way to recover the configuration without manual database intervention.
fix: Use soft delete with isActive flag instead of permanent deletion.

title: Hero slide hard delete with translation cascade risk
description: HeroSlide records are permanently deleted. If translations exist in separate tables, they may be orphaned or cascade-deleted, losing localized content across all 22 languages.
fix: Use soft delete. Verify that related HeroSlideTranslation records are handled correctly.

title: Shipping zone hard delete could break active shipping calculations
description: ShippingZone records are permanently deleted. Active orders using this zone's rates may fail at fulfillment. No pre-flight check for orders referencing this zone.
fix: Soft delete. Before deletion, check if any pending/processing orders reference this shipping zone.

title: Loyalty tier hard delete could reset customer statuses
description: LoyaltyTierConfig records are permanently deleted. Customers assigned to this tier will have a dangling reference. The loyalty system may malfunction for affected customers.
fix: Soft delete. Before deletion, reassign customers in the deleted tier to a default tier. Prevent deletion if customers are assigned.

title: Email account hard delete breaks email sending capability
description: EmailAccount records (SMTP/API credentials) are permanently deleted. If this is the only configured sending account, the system loses email delivery capability. No fallback check.
fix: Soft delete. Before deletion, verify at least one other active email account exists. Warn if this is the last account.

title: Email automation flow hard delete stops active automations
description: EmailAutomationFlow records are permanently deleted. If users are currently in the flow's pipeline, their automation will halt mid-sequence with no recovery.
fix: Soft delete. Drain the flow (complete or skip remaining steps for enrolled users) before deactivating.

title: Community seed route is a mutation endpoint without Zod validation
description: The /api/community/seed endpoint is a POST handler that likely creates test/seed data. Without Zod validation, it could accept malicious payloads. Seed endpoints should not exist in production.
fix: Either delete this endpoint or gate it behind NODE_ENV === 'development' check AND withAdminGuard. Add Zod validation for any accepted parameters.

title: Referral code generation without Zod validation
description: POST handler generates referral codes without input validation. Could allow abuse to generate unlimited referral codes or codes with injected content.
fix: Add Zod validation. Rate limit to prevent code generation abuse (e.g., 5 codes per user per hour).

title: Avatar upload without Zod validation on metadata
description: The DELETE handler in this route destroys avatar data. File upload endpoints should validate metadata (filename, content-type, dimensions) via Zod schema.
fix: Add Zod schema for upload metadata validation. Validate file type (only images), size (< 5MB), and dimensions.

title: SEO sitemap generation without validation
description: POST handler triggers sitemap regeneration without Zod validation. Could be abused to repeatedly trigger expensive database scans for sitemap generation.
fix: Add rate limiting (1 request per 5 minutes). Add Zod validation for any configuration parameters.

title: Translation night worker without input validation
description: POST handler triggers translation processing without Zod validation. Could be abused to trigger expensive translation operations repeatedly.
fix: Add Zod schema for parameters (target languages, scope). Add rate limiting.
